title: The next era of Atlantic hurricanes could be far more destructive
content: Recent research suggests climate change could supercharge swings between quiet years and hyperactive seasons packed with dangerous storms.
snippet: Recent research suggests climate change could supercharge swings between quiet years and hyperactive seasons packed with dangerous storms.

title: Quantifying uncertainty in climate change impacts: A Bayesian approach for the Cagayan river basin
content: This study develops a probabilistic flood-impact assessment for the Cagayan river basin (northern Luzon, Philippines) by linking Bayesian extreme-value inference with uncertainty propagation along the rainfall–flood–damage chain.
snippet: This study develops a probabilistic flood-impact assessment for the Cagayan river basin (northern Luzon, Philippines) by linking Bayesian extreme-value inference with uncertainty propagation along the

title: As floods get worse, Britain tries a new solution: beavers
content: Scientists have enlisted some of the animal kingdom's best flood engineers — beavers — to help.
snippet: Scientists have enlisted some of the animal kingdom's best flood engineers — beavers — to help.

title: African cities use nature to fight floods and climate change
content: From Kinshasa to Dire Dawa, cities across Africa are discovering that wetlands, trees and parks could be their strongest defense against climate change.
snippet: From Kinshasa to Dire Dawa, cities across Africa are discovering that wetlands, trees and parks could be their strongest defense against climate change.

title: The fiery tornadoes that could mop up oil spills
content: More than 15 years after the Deepwater Horizon disaster, researchers are still searching for new ways to tackle disastrous spills. Some are looking to flaming twisters.
snippet: More than 15 years after the Deepwater Horizon disaster, researchers are still searching for new ways to tackle disastrous spills. Some are looking to flaming twisters.

title: Seeing the hazard: Effects of visual communication in flood warning messages
content: The study examines how visual cues, especially maps and warning symbols, can improve flood early warning messages by helping citizens understand event magnitude, timing, impacts and recommended protective actions.
snippet: The study examines how visual cues, especially maps and warning symbols, can improve flood early warning messages by helping citizens understand event magnitude, timing, impacts and recommended protec

title: The value of forecasters-in-the-loop in real-time flood forecasting in the age of machine learning
content: ML models for hydrological forecasting, even when given perfect inputs, cannot yet match the reliability of experienced human forecasters operating real-world prediction systems.
snippet: ML models for hydrological forecasting, even when given perfect inputs, cannot yet match the reliability of experienced human forecasters operating real-world prediction systems.

title: Disaster risk financing in the Pacific: a landscape study of systems, instruments, and enabling conditions
content: This working paper provides a comprehensive analysis of the disaster risk financing landscape across the Pacific.
snippet: This working paper provides a comprehensive analysis of the disaster risk financing landscape across the Pacific.

title: Subsidence more than doubles sea-level rise today along densely populated coasts
content: This study addresses the problem of low confidence in estimates of vertical land motion (VLM) and its contribution to relative sea-level (RSL) rise.
snippet: This study addresses the problem of low confidence in estimates of vertical land motion (VLM) and its contribution to relative sea-level (RSL) rise.

title: Floods with compounding hazards increased almost threefold in 30 years
content: Average economic losses from floods with compounding hazards are almost three times higher than losses from floods alone.
snippet: Average economic losses from floods with compounding hazards are almost three times higher than losses from floods alone.

title: India’s future climate resilience will depend on its ability to build a public healthcare system that recognises heat as a highly gendered disaster
content: Women experience heat differently due to physiological, occupational, and socio-economic factors; they cannot be considered an “invisible category” within climate governance
snippet: Women experience heat differently due to physiological, occupational, and socio-economic factors; they cannot be considered an “invisible category” within climate governance

title: RFP-Ghana Resilient Infrastructure Strategy-180526
content: Hiring a consultancy firm to support the development of the National Strategy for Resilient Infrastructure in Ghana
snippet: Hiring a consultancy firm to support the development of the National Strategy for Resilient Infrastructure in Ghana

title: State of the Climate in Latin America and the Caribbean 2025
content: The State of the Climate in the Latin America and Caribbean report provides details of extreme weather and climate change impacts in the entire region, from the Amazon to the Andes, and from coastal waters to snowy peaks
snippet: The State of the Climate in the Latin America and Caribbean report provides details of extreme weather and climate change impacts in the entire region, from the Amazon to the Andes, and from coastal w

title: ‘Preventive engineering’ urged to reduce summer storm damage
content: Loans to help owners strengthen houses a better use of money than compensation after the fact.
snippet: Loans to help owners strengthen houses a better use of money than compensation after the fact.

title: Ready of not? Hurricane preparedness, response, and recovery of farms, forests, and rural communities in the U.S. Caribbean
content: This study highlights that forest owners, and rural communities in the Caribbean are among the groups most vulnerable to hurricanes and other extreme climate events.
snippet: This study highlights that forest owners, and rural communities in the Caribbean are among the groups most vulnerable to hurricanes and other extreme climate events.

title: Community risk assessment of flooding and heat hazards available for Baltimore
content: Chesapeake communities are at a heightened and increasing risk for flooding and coastal hazards.
snippet: Chesapeake communities are at a heightened and increasing risk for flooding and coastal hazards.

title: The hantavirus outbreak is the warning the world needs to improve pandemic preparedness
content: The latest case of a Canadian passenger testing positive shows the hantavirus outbreak isn’t over yet. We can probably expect more cases, given the long incubation period of this infection.
snippet: The latest case of a Canadian passenger testing positive shows the hantavirus outbreak isn’t over yet. We can probably expect more cases, given the long incubation period of this infection.

title: Radiotherapy continuity for cancer treatment: Lessons learned from natural disasters
content: This study examines the impact of natural disasters on the continuity of radiotherapy services, a critical component of cancer treatment responsible for managing one of the world's leading causes of death.
snippet: This study examines the impact of natural disasters on the continuity of radiotherapy services, a critical component of cancer treatment responsible for managing one of the world's leading causes of d

title: Characterizing changes in postfire debris-flow hazard as burned areas recover
content: Updating post-fire debris-flow hazard thresholds with satellite-tracked vegetation recovery reduces false alarms and improves predictions for years 2 and 3 after a wildfire.
snippet: Updating post-fire debris-flow hazard thresholds with satellite-tracked vegetation recovery reduces false alarms and improves predictions for years 2 and 3 after a wildfire.

title: Are the poor more exposed to climate hazards in Latin America?
content: This paper addresses two main questions. First, what proportion of people are exposed to climate hazards in Latin America and the Caribbean, especially among the poor versus the nonpoor?
snippet: This paper addresses two main questions. First, what proportion of people are exposed to climate hazards in Latin America and the Caribbean, especially among the poor versus the nonpoor?

title: Women experience extreme heat differently to men. And they’re adapting to it in creative ways
content: This research review, which looks at examples across Oceania, Africa and Asia finds that the people who are forced to adapt to the greatest extent are the ones that climate policies ignore the most.
snippet: This research review, which looks at examples across Oceania, Africa and Asia finds that the people who are forced to adapt to the greatest extent are the ones that climate policies ignore the most.

title: Disaster risk financing: Annual report 2025
content: The report provides deep dives into the DRF programmes in each country, as well as stories from the field, interviews with partners and insights into how WFP promotes the use of pre-arranged financing.
snippet: The report provides deep dives into the DRF programmes in each country, as well as stories from the field, interviews with partners and insights into how WFP promotes the use of pre-arranged financing

title: A swarm of earthquakes in South Africa’s Karoo Basin poses questions for oil and gas development
content: A recent study cautions that the Karoo, a potential target for shale gas exploration, might not be as seismologically calm as it appears.
snippet: A recent study cautions that the Karoo, a potential target for shale gas exploration, might not be as seismologically calm as it appears.

title: Sea level rise is accelerating, scientists confirm
content: Human-driven climate change is driving the rise of sea levels, worsening flood conditions and threatening coastal communities around the world. Not only is sea level rising, but it’s rising faster every year.
snippet: Human-driven climate change is driving the rise of sea levels, worsening flood conditions and threatening coastal communities around the world. Not only is sea level rising, but it’s rising faster eve

title: Vanuatu Marks First International Day in Memory of the Victims of Earthquakes
content: On 29 April 2026, Vanuatu marked its first national observance of the International Day in Memory of the Victims of Earthquakes, bringing together partners to strengthen earthquake preparedness and disaster resilience.
snippet: On 29 April 2026, Vanuatu marked its first national observance of the International Day in Memory of the Victims of Earthquakes, bringing together partners to strengthen earthquake preparedness and di

title: Disability-inclusive disaster risk reduction in Amadora: building resilience through local action
content: Building resilience for people with disabilities requires ensuring that their needs are fully recognised and integrated into disaster risk reduction (DRR) planning.
snippet: Building resilience for people with disabilities requires ensuring that their needs are fully recognised and integrated into disaster risk reduction (DRR) planning.

title: After the flames, wildfires pollute drinking water for years
content: When people think about wildfires, they usually think about flames, smoke and evacuations. However, for many communities, some of the most important damage begins after the fire has passed.
snippet: When people think about wildfires, they usually think about flames, smoke and evacuations. However, for many communities, some of the most important damage begins after the fire has passed.

title: Where extreme heat could threaten the World Cup, endangering players and fans
content: The upcoming FIFA World Cup is expected to be the most-watched sporting event in history, with more than 5 million people slated to attend an expanded competition hosted across the United States, Canada and Mexico.
snippet: The upcoming FIFA World Cup is expected to be the most-watched sporting event in history, with more than 5 million people slated to attend an expanded competition hosted across the United States, Cana

title: Morocco’s whole-of-government approach to climate policy in action
content: After facing a series of climate shocks and droughts in 2018-2023 and multiple policy dialogues with international partners, Morocco has placed climate change high on its policy agenda.
snippet: After facing a series of climate shocks and droughts in 2018-2023 and multiple policy dialogues with international partners, Morocco has placed climate change high on its policy agenda.

title: Record-setting retreat of Hektoria glacier
content: A team of scientists published an analysis of Hektoria’s collapse based on a suite of remote-sensing data, finding that its particular geometry enabled the rapid change.
snippet: A team of scientists published an analysis of Hektoria’s collapse based on a suite of remote-sensing data, finding that its particular geometry enabled the rapid change.

title: Understanding the hazard potential of the Seattle fault zone: It’s “pretty close to home”
content: A new study in GSA Bulletin seeks to constrain how often and where faults cause earthquakes under the heart of Seattle
snippet: A new study in GSA Bulletin seeks to constrain how often and where faults cause earthquakes under the heart of Seattle

title: US-Indian space mission maps extreme subsidence in Mexico City
content: Home to some 20 million people, the Mexico City area is built atop an aquifer. Extensive groundwater pumping, combined with the weight of urban development, has resulted in the compaction of the ancient lakebed beneath the city for more than a century.
snippet: Home to some 20 million people, the Mexico City area is built atop an aquifer. Extensive groundwater pumping, combined with the weight of urban development, has resulted in the compaction of the ancie

title: Study reveals how Himalayan storms moistens the upper atmosphere
content: A recent study has uncovered a detailed mechanism through which intense storms over the Himalayas contribute to increasing moisture in the lower stratosphere—a layer of the atmosphere crucial to global climate regulation
snippet: A recent study has uncovered a detailed mechanism through which intense storms over the Himalayas contribute to increasing moisture in the lower stratosphere—a layer of the atmosphere crucial to globa

title: Extreme weather events accelerating tidal wetland loss
content: Normally after a storm, tidal wetlands can recover by themselves. Due to the increased frequency &  intensity of hurricanes, they are losing that recovery capacity. So after a hurricane, there is need to provide a proactive management plan to help recover
snippet: Normally after a storm, tidal wetlands can recover by themselves. Due to the increased frequency & intensity of hurricanes, they are losing that recovery capacity. So after a hurricane, there is n

title: Adaptation through water: Scaling private sector finance for climate adaptation in Southeast Asia
content: This report explores how the private sector can accelerate participation and financing for Adaptation through Water (AtW). It highlights the key enablers of bankable AtW solutions globally, including translating resilience into financial value.
snippet: This report explores how the private sector can accelerate participation and financing for Adaptation through Water (AtW). It highlights the key enablers of bankable AtW solutions globally, including

title: Climate extremes and socioeconomic impacts of extreme rainfall events in April and May 2024 in Rio Grande do Sul, Brazil
content: This study evaluates observed changes in temperature and precipitation extremes across the state and examines the May 2024 floods as a case study of evolving risk conditions in southern Brazil.
snippet: This study evaluates observed changes in temperature and precipitation extremes across the state and examines the May 2024 floods as a case study of evolving risk conditions in southern Brazil.

title: Toward enhanced coastal flooding forecasting using deterministic and probabilistic models
content: This study focuses on the application of advanced data‐driven modeling techniques to assess and predict high‐tide flooding (HTF) risks in Oman. The research analyzes the duration, frequency, and economic impacts of HTF.
snippet: This study focuses on the application of advanced data‐driven modeling techniques to assess and predict high‐tide flooding (HTF) risks in Oman. The research analyzes the duration, frequency, and econo

title: Cybersecurity in ports: Shifting from risk to resilience
content: This report examines the growing cybersecurity risks ports face. Drawing on a global survey of 109 port stakeholders it provides a comprehensive overview of cybersecurity policies, practices, and barriers across port ecosystems worldwide
snippet: This report examines the growing cybersecurity risks ports face. Drawing on a global survey of 109 port stakeholders it provides a comprehensive overview of cybersecurity policies, practices, and barr

title: Strengthening the disaster resilience of SMEs in Sendai
content: This landscape study assesses how SMEs in Sendai city  perceive climate change and disaster risks and how they assess the resilience of their companies and the cities in which they operate.
snippet: This landscape study assesses how SMEs in Sendai city perceive climate change and disaster risks and how they assess the resilience of their companies and the cities in which they operate.

title: Daily Herald
content: The Daily Herald covers the news you need to know in Chicago's suburbs.
snippet: The Daily Herald covers the news you need to know in Chicago's suburbs.
